text.title: C# .NET Core Backend Failure Modes
text.summary: 7 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Host process crashes silently mid-request; logs show `Unobserved task exception` with no stack frame in the codebase.
antipattern.root-cause: `async void` swallows the exception and tears down the process; the only legitimate use is a top-level event handler.
antipattern.fix: Change return type to `async Task`. Run a Roslyn analyzer (`VSTHRD100`) at CI to block reintroduction.

antipattern.symptom: Endpoint deadlocks under load; thread-pool starvation visible in `dotnet-counters`.
antipattern.root-cause: Author called `.Result` or `.Wait()` on an async API to avoid making the caller `async`.
antipattern.fix: Propagate `async` all the way up. Reviewers must reject any new `.Result`/`.Wait()`. Roslyn analyzer `VSTHRD002` enforces this.

antipattern.symptom: `BackgroundService` throws `InvalidOperationException: Cannot consume scoped service 'AppDbContext'` on first iteration — or worse, silently reuses one DbContext for the process lifetime.
antipattern.root-cause: Author injected `AppDbContext` into a singleton hosted service constructor.
antipattern.fix: Inject `IServiceScopeFactory` instead; create a scope per work unit inside `ExecuteAsync` (`using var scope = _scopeFactory.CreateScope();`).

antipattern.root-cause: Author forgot `await` on `ToListAsync()`/`FirstOrDefaultAsync()` and returned the Task.
antipattern.fix: Set `<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>` and enable `CS4014` to fail the build on un-awaited Tasks.

antipattern.symptom: Dev migration drops a column that prod still depends on; outage during next deploy.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent ran `dotnet ef database update` against a shared dev/prod DB without a human checkpoint.
antipattern.fix: Generate the SQL via `dotnet ef migrations script` for human review; gate destructive operations behind a confirmation prompt.

antipattern.symptom: Source-generated mapper (Mapperly) build error: `partial keyword required`.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent forgot the `partial` modifier on the mapper class declaration.
antipattern.fix: Add `partial` to the class declaration. Add a Roslyn analyzer or template snippet to enforce.

antipattern.symptom: Endpoint throws `AutoMapperMappingException: Missing type map` at first request after deploy.
antipattern.root-cause: `AddAutoMapper(...)` was called without the assembly that contains the `Profile`s.
antipattern.fix: Use `services.AddAutoMapper(typeof(Program).Assembly)` and assert `MapperConfiguration.AssertConfigurationIsValid()` in a unit test.
